pages.text: $(bold)example_multiblock$() is registered in Java (see $(italic)ExamplePattern$()) rather than through a datapack, so it can attach custom behavior when it forms and breaks. Its pattern uses two 3x3 layers:

Bottom layer: $(bold)Example Multiblock Part$() blocks in a plus shape, with a $(bold)Gold Block$() on one edge.

Top layer: identical, except the center Part block is replaced by the $(bold)Example Multiblock Controller$() - the core.

Because the controller is also the activation symbol, you build every other block first and place the controller last to complete the structure - no wrench needed, though the wrench works too since this pattern allows both automatic and wrench-triggered formation. The structure also accepts being rotated to any of the 4 horizontal facings.

$(bold)On formation:$() a lightning bolt strikes the core block's position (server-side only, purely visual/audio flair - it deals no damage in this example).

$(bold)On breaking:$() any block being removed from the structure breaks it; every player within 30 blocks gets a chat message announcing the collapse, and a glass-break sound plays at the core.

This entry has no interactive multiblock preview because the pattern's two layers are easiest to understand as a diagram - build the plus-shaped ring first, then close it off by placing the $(bold)Example Multiblock Controller$() in the center of the top layer.
